295→
   296→//	Change the following constants to affect the look of the omega cannon.
   297→//	Changing these constants will not affect the damage done.
   298→//	WARNING: If you change DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST and MAX_OMEGA_BLOBS, you don't merely change the look of the cannon,
   299→//	you change its range.  If you decrease DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST, you decrease how far the gun can fire.
   300→#define	MIN_OMEGA_BLOBS		3				//	No matter how close the obstruction, at this many blobs created.
   301→#define	MIN_OMEGA_DIST			(F1_0*3)		//	At least this distance between blobs, unless doing so would violate MIN_OMEGA_BLOBS
   302→#define	DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST	(F1_0*5)		//	This is the desired distance between blobs.  For distances > MIN_OMEGA_BLOBS*DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST, but not very large, this will apply.
   303→#define	MAX_OMEGA_BLOBS		16				//	No matter how far away the obstruction, this is the maximum number of blobs.
   304→#define	MAX_OMEGA_DIST			(MAX_OMEGA_BLOBS * DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST)		//	Maximum extent of lightning blobs.
   305→
   306→//	Additionally, several constants which apply to homing objects in general control the behavior of the Omega Cannon.
   307→//	They are defined in laser.h.  They are copied here for reference.  These values are valid on 1/10/96:
   308→//	If you want the Omega Cannon view cone to be different than the Homing Missile viewcone, contact MK to make the change.
   309→//	(Unless you are a programmer, in which case, do it yourself!)
   310→#define	OMEGA_MIN_TRACKABLE_DOT			(15*F1_0/16)		//	Larger values mean narrower cone.  F1_0 means damn near impossible.  0 means 180 degree field of view.
   311→#define	OMEGA_MAX_TRACKABLE_DIST		MAX_OMEGA_DIST	//	An object must be at least this close to be tracked.
   312→
   313→//	Note, you don't need to change these constants.  You can control damage and energy consumption by changing the
   314→//	usual bitmaps.tbl parameters.
   315→#define	OMEGA_DAMAGE_SCALE			32				//	Controls how much damage is done.  This gets multiplied by FrameTime and then again by the damage specified in bitmaps.tbl in the $WEAPON line.
   316→#define	OMEGA_ENERGY_CONSUMPTION	16				//	Controls how much energy is consumed.  This gets multiplied by FrameTime and then again by the energy parameter from bitmaps.tbl.
   317→//	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   318→
   319→void delete_old_omega_blobs(object *parent_objp)
   320→{
   321→#ifdef USE_OX_BRIDGE
   322→	{
   323→		static bool registered = false;
   324→		if (!registered) {

1→(* Pure OCaml game logic for laser/missile functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d2/laser.cpp and main_d1/laser.cpp *)
     3→
     4→let f1_0 = 0x10000
     5→
     6→(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)
     7→
     8→type _ Effect.t +=
     9→  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
    10→  | Fetch_object_pos : int -> (int * int * int) Effect.t
    11→      (* objnum -> (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z) *)
    12→  | Laser_create_new :
    13→      (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int)
    14→      -> int Effect.t
    15→      (* (dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, segnum, parent_objnum,
    16→          objtype, make_sound) -> objnum or -1 *)
    17→  | Set_laser_track_goal : (int * int) -> unit Effect.t
    18→      (* (objnum, goal_obj) *)
    19→  | Fetch_weapon_create_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    20→      (* weapon_type -> [|render_type; blob_size; model_num;
    21→         po_len_to_width_ratio; mass; drag; bounce; bounce_cheat;
    22→         polygon_model_rad|] *)
    23→  | Obj_create_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
    24→      (* (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, laser_radius, rtype)
    25→         -> objnum *)
    26→  | Set_weapon_obj_props : int array -> unit Effect.t
    27→      (* [|objnum; model_num; size; mass; drag; phys_flags_or|]
    28→         model_num < 0 means skip polymodel setup
    29→         phys_flags_or is OR'd into existing flags *)
    30→  | Fetch_release_guided_missile_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    31→      (* player_num -> [| Player_num; guided_is_null; newdemo_state; game_mode_and_gm_multi |] *)
    32→  | Release_guided_set_viewer : int -> unit Effect.t
    33→      (* player_num -> sets Missile_viewer = Guided_missile[player_num] *)
    34→  | Release_guided_multi_send : int -> unit Effect.t
    35→      (* player_num -> multi_send_guided_info(Guided_missile[player_num], 1) *)
    36→  | Release_guided_newdemo_record : unit Effect.t
    37→      (* newdemo_record_guided_end() *)
    38→  | Release_guided_clear : int -> unit Effect.t
    39→      (* player_num -> Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
    40→  | Fetch_omega_blob_objnums : int -> int array Effect.t
    41→      (* parent_num -> array of objnums matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num) *)
    42→  | Obj_delete : int -> unit Effect.t
    43→      (* objnum -> deletes the object *)
    44→
    45→(* make_random_vector: generate a random unit-ish vector using P_Rand.
    46→   Same logic as C make_random_vector / ox_controlcen.ml *)
    47→let make_random_vector () =
    48→  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
    49→  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    50→  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    51→  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
    52→  v
    53→;;
    54→
    55→(* create_homing_missile: Create a homing missile aimed at goal_obj.
    56→   If goal_obj == -1, fire in a random direction.
    57→   D1 and D2 versions are functionally identical.
    58→   C original: laser.cpp create_homing_missile *)
    59→let create_homing_missile ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
    60→    ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound =
    61→  let vector_to_goal =
    62→    if goal_obj = -1 then
    63→      make_random_vector ()
    64→    else begin
    65→      let goal_x, goal_y, goal_z = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_pos goal_obj) in
    66→      (* D2 uses vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick = sub + normalize_quick
    67→         D1 uses vm_vec_sub + vm_vec_normalize_quick — same result *)
    68→      let _, dir =
    69→        Ox_math.vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick
    70→          ~v_end:(goal_x, goal_y, goal_z)
    71→          ~v_start:(objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z)
    72→      in
    73→      let random_vector = make_random_vector () in
    74→      let blended =
    75→        Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:dir ~src:random_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
    76→      in
    77→      let _, normalized = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:blended in
    78→      normalized
    79→    end
    80→  in
    81→  let dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = vector_to_goal in
    82→  let objnum =
    83→    Effect.perform
    84→      (Laser_create_new
    85→         ( dir_x, dir_y, dir_z
    86→         , objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z
    87→         , objp_segnum, objp_objnum, objtype, make_sound ))
    88→  in
    89→  if objnum = -1 then -1
    90→  else begin
    91→    Effect.perform (Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj));
    92→    objnum
    93→  end
    94→;;
    95→
    96→(* -- Constants for create_weapon_object ----------------------------------- *)
    97→
    98→let weapon_render_none = -1
    99→let weapon_render_laser = 0
   100→let weapon_render_blob = 1

1→(* Bridge adapter for laser/missile functions.
     2→   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effect externals *)
     5→external effect_fetch_object_pos
     6→  :  int
     7→  -> int array
     8→  = "cd_ox_effect_laser_fetch_object_pos"
     9→
    10→external effect_laser_create_new
    11→  :  int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int
    12→  -> int
    13→  = "cd_ox_effect_laser_create_new_bytecode"
    14→    "cd_ox_effect_laser_create_new"
    15→
    16→external effect_set_laser_track_goal
    17→  :  int -> int
    18→  -> unit
    19→  = "cd_ox_effect_laser_set_track_goal"
    20→
    21→external effect_p_rand : unit -> int = "cd_ox_effect_p_rand"
    22→
    23→external effect_fetch_weapon_create_data
    24→  :  int
    25→  -> int array
    26→  = "cd_ox_effect_fetch_weapon_create_data"
    27→
    28→external effect_obj_create_weapon
    29→  :  int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int
    30→  -> int
    31→  = "cd_ox_effect_obj_create_weapon_bytecode"
    32→    "cd_ox_effect_obj_create_weapon"
    33→
    34→external effect_set_weapon_obj_props
    35→  :  int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int
    36→  -> unit
    37→  = "cd_ox_effect_set_weapon_obj_props_bytecode"
    38→    "cd_ox_effect_set_weapon_obj_props"
    39→
    40→external effect_fetch_release_guided_missile_data
    41→  :  int
    42→  -> int array
    43→  = "cd_ox_effect_fetch_release_guided_missile_data"
    44→
    45→external effect_release_guided_set_viewer
    46→  :  int
    47→  -> unit
    48→  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_set_viewer"
    49→
    50→external effect_release_guided_multi_send
    51→  :  int
    52→  -> unit
    53→  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_multi_send"
    54→
    55→external effect_release_guided_newdemo_record
    56→  :  unit
    57→  -> unit
    58→  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_newdemo_record"
    59→
    60→external effect_release_guided_clear
    61→  :  int
    62→  -> unit
    63→  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_clear"
    64→
    65→external effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums
    66→  :  int
    67→  -> int array
    68→  = "cd_ox_effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums"
    69→
    70→external effect_obj_delete
    71→  :  int
    72→  -> unit
    73→  = "cd_ox_effect_obj_delete"
    74→
    75→(* Effect handler *)
    76→let effc (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) : ((a, 'b) Effect.Deep.continuation -> 'b) option =
    77→  match eff with
    78→  | Ox_laser.P_Rand ->
    79→    Some (fun k -> Effect.Deep.continue k (effect_p_rand ()))
    80→  | Ox_laser.Fetch_object_pos objnum ->
    81→    Some (fun k ->
    82→      let arr = effect_fetch_object_pos objnum in
    83→      Effect.Deep.continue k (arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2)))
    84→  | Ox_laser.Laser_create_new (dx, dy, dz, px, py, pz, segnum, parent, objtype, make_sound) ->
    85→    Some (fun k ->
    86→      let objnum = effect_laser_create_new dx dy dz px py pz segnum parent objtype make_sound in
    87→      Effect.Deep.continue k objnum)
    88→  | Ox_laser.Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj) ->
    89→    Some (fun k ->
    90→      effect_set_laser_track_goal objnum goal_obj;
    91→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    92→  | Ox_laser.Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type ->
    93→    Some (fun k ->
    94→      let arr = effect_fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type in
    95→      Effect.Deep.continue k arr)
    96→  | Ox_laser.Obj_create_weapon (wtype, segnum, px, py, pz, radius, rtype) ->
    97→    Some (fun k ->
    98→      let objnum = effect_obj_create_weapon wtype segnum px py pz radius rtype in
    99→      Effect.Deep.continue k objnum)
   100→  | Ox_laser.Set_weapon_obj_props arr ->
   101→    Some (fun k ->
   102→      effect_set_weapon_obj_props arr.(0) arr.(1) arr.(2) arr.(3) arr.(4) arr.(5);
   103→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   104→  | Ox_laser.Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num ->
   105→    Some (fun k ->
   106→      let arr = effect_fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num in
   107→      Effect.Deep.continue k arr)
   108→  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_set_viewer player_num ->
   109→    Some (fun k ->
   110→      effect_release_guided_set_viewer player_num;
   111→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   112→  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_multi_send player_num ->
   113→    Some (fun k ->
   114→      effect_release_guided_multi_send player_num;
   115→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   116→  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_newdemo_record ->
   117→    Some (fun k ->
   118→      effect_release_guided_newdemo_record ();
   119→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   120→  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_clear player_num ->
   121→    Some (fun k ->
   122→      effect_release_guided_clear player_num;
   123→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   124→  | Ox_laser.Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num ->
   125→    Some (fun k ->
   126→      let arr = effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num in
   127→      Effect.Deep.continue k arr)
   128→  | Ox_laser.Obj_delete objnum ->
   129→    Some (fun k ->
   130→      effect_obj_delete objnum;
   131→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   132→  | _ -> None
   133→;;
   134→
   135→let cd_create_homing_missile objp_pos_x objp_pos_y objp_pos_z
   136→    objp_segnum objp_objnum goal_obj objtype make_sound =
   137→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   138→    (fun () ->
   139→      Ox_laser.create_homing_missile
   140→        ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
   141→        ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound)
   142→    ()
   143→    { retc = (fun r -> r)
   144→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   145→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] create_homing_missile exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   146→        Out_channel.flush stderr;
   147→        -1)
   148→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
   149→    }
   150→;;

1→(* Pure OCaml game logic for laser/missile functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d2/laser.cpp and main_d1/laser.cpp *)
     3→
     4→let f1_0 = 0x10000
     5→
     6→(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)
     7→
     8→type _ Effect.t +=
     9→  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
    10→  | Fetch_object_pos : int -> (int * int * int) Effect.t
    11→      (* objnum -> (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z) *)
    12→  | Laser_create_new :
    13→      (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int)
    14→      -> int Effect.t
    15→      (* (dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, segnum, parent_objnum,
    16→          objtype, make_sound) -> objnum or -1 *)
    17→  | Set_laser_track_goal : (int * int) -> unit Effect.t
    18→      (* (objnum, goal_obj) *)
    19→  | Fetch_weapon_create_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    20→      (* weapon_type -> [|render_type; blob_size; model_num;
    21→         po_len_to_width_ratio; mass; drag; bounce; bounce_cheat;
    22→         polygon_model_rad|] *)
    23→  | Obj_create_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
    24→      (* (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, laser_radius, rtype)
    25→         -> objnum *)
    26→  | Set_weapon_obj_props : int array -> unit Effect.t
    27→      (* [|objnum; model_num; size; mass; drag; phys_flags_or|]
    28→         model_num < 0 means skip polymodel setup
    29→         phys_flags_or is OR'd into existing flags *)
    30→  | Fetch_release_guided_missile_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    31→      (* player_num -> [| Player_num; guided_is_null; newdemo_state; game_mode_and_gm_multi |] *)
    32→  | Release_guided_set_viewer : int -> unit Effect.t
    33→      (* player_num -> sets Missile_viewer = Guided_missile[player_num] *)
    34→  | Release_guided_multi_send : int -> unit Effect.t
    35→      (* player_num -> multi_send_guided_info(Guided_missile[player_num], 1) *)
    36→  | Release_guided_newdemo_record : unit Effect.t
    37→      (* newdemo_record_guided_end() *)
    38→  | Release_guided_clear : int -> unit Effect.t
    39→      (* player_num -> Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
    40→  | Fetch_omega_blob_objnums : int -> int array Effect.t
    41→      (* parent_num -> array of objnums matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num) *)
    42→  | Obj_delete : int -> unit Effect.t
    43→      (* objnum -> deletes the object *)
    44→
    45→(* make_random_vector: generate a random unit-ish vector using P_Rand.
    46→   Same logic as C make_random_vector / ox_controlcen.ml *)
    47→let make_random_vector () =
    48→  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
    49→  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    50→  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    51→  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
    52→  v
    53→;;
    54→
    55→(* create_homing_missile: Create a homing missile aimed at goal_obj.
    56→   If goal_obj == -1, fire in a random direction.
    57→   D1 and D2 versions are functionally identical.
    58→   C original: laser.cpp create_homing_missile *)
    59→let create_homing_missile ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
    60→    ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound =
    61→  let vector_to_goal =
    62→    if goal_obj = -1 then
    63→      make_random_vector ()
    64→    else begin
    65→      let goal_x, goal_y, goal_z = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_pos goal_obj) in
    66→      (* D2 uses vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick = sub + normalize_quick
    67→         D1 uses vm_vec_sub + vm_vec_normalize_quick — same result *)
    68→      let _, dir =
    69→        Ox_math.vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick
    70→          ~v_end:(goal_x, goal_y, goal_z)
    71→          ~v_start:(objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z)
    72→      in
    73→      let random_vector = make_random_vector () in
    74→      let blended =
    75→        Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:dir ~src:random_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
    76→      in
    77→      let _, normalized = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:blended in
    78→      normalized
    79→    end
    80→  in
    81→  let dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = vector_to_goal in
    82→  let objnum =
    83→    Effect.perform
    84→      (Laser_create_new
    85→         ( dir_x, dir_y, dir_z
    86→         , objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z
    87→         , objp_segnum, objp_objnum, objtype, make_sound ))
    88→  in
    89→  if objnum = -1 then -1
    90→  else begin
    91→    Effect.perform (Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj));
    92→    objnum
    93→  end
    94→;;
    95→
    96→(* -- Constants for create_weapon_object ----------------------------------- *)
    97→
    98→let weapon_render_none = -1
    99→let weapon_render_laser = 0
   100→let weapon_render_blob = 1
   101→let weapon_render_polymodel = 2
   102→let weapon_render_vclip = 3
   103→
   104→let rt_none = 0
   105→let rt_polyobj = 1
   106→let rt_laser = 3
   107→let rt_weapon_vclip = 7
   108→
   109→let pf_bounce = 0x04
   110→let pf_bounces_twice = 0x200
   111→
   112→(* create_weapon_object: Create a weapon object with the right render type,
   113→   size, physics, and bounce properties.
   114→   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp create_weapon_object *)
   115→let create_weapon_object ~weapon_type ~segnum ~pos_x ~pos_y ~pos_z =
   116→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type) in
   117→  let render_type = data.(0) in
   118→  let blob_size = data.(1) in
   119→  let model_num = data.(2) in
   120→  let po_len_to_width_ratio = data.(3) in
   121→  let mass = data.(4) in
   122→  let drag = data.(5) in
   123→  let bounce = data.(6) in
   124→  let bounce_cheat = data.(7) in
   125→  let polygon_model_rad = data.(8) in
   126→
   127→  let rtype = ref (-1) in
   128→  let laser_radius = ref (-1) in
   129→
   130→  (match render_type with
   131→   | r when r = weapon_render_blob ->
   132→     rtype := rt_laser;
   133→     laser_radius := blob_size
   134→   | r when r = weapon_render_polymodel ->
   135→     laser_radius := 0;
   136→     rtype := rt_polyobj
   137→   | r when r = weapon_render_laser ->
   138→     () (* Int3() - not supported anymore *)
   139→   | r when r = weapon_render_none ->
   140→     rtype := rt_none;
   141→     laser_radius := f1_0
   142→   | r when r = weapon_render_vclip ->
   143→     rtype := rt_weapon_vclip;
   144→     laser_radius := blob_size
   145→   | _ ->
   146→     failwith "Invalid weapon render type in Laser_create_new");
   147→
   148→  (* Assert(laser_radius != -1); Assert(rtype != -1); *)
   149→
   150→  let objnum = Effect.perform
   151→    (Obj_create_weapon
   152→       (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, !laser_radius, !rtype))
   153→  in
   154→
   155→  (* Compute size for polymodel weapons *)
   156→  let final_size =
   157→    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then
   158→      Ox_math.fixdiv ~a:polygon_model_rad ~b:po_len_to_width_ratio
   159→    else
   160→      0 (* not used — obj_create already set size to laser_radius *)
   161→  in
   162→
   163→  let final_model_num =
   164→    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then model_num else -1
   165→  in
   166→
   167→  (* Compute phys_flags to OR in *)
   168→  let phys_flags = ref 0 in
   169→  if bounce = 1 then
   170→    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor pf_bounce;
   171→  if bounce = 2 || bounce_cheat <> 0 then
   172→    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor (pf_bounce + pf_bounces_twice);
   173→
   174→  Effect.perform
   175→    (Set_weapon_obj_props
   176→       [| objnum; final_model_num; final_size; mass; drag; !phys_flags |]);
   177→
   178→  objnum
   179→;;
   180→
   181→(* ND_STATE_RECORDING = 1 *)
   182→let nd_state_recording = 1
   183→
   184→(* release_guided_missile: give up control of the guided missile.
   185→   C original: laser.cpp release_guided_missile *)
   186→let release_guided_missile ~player_num =
   187→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num) in
   188→  let player_num_global = data.(0) in
   189→  let guided_is_null = data.(1) <> 0 in
   190→  let newdemo_state = data.(2) in
   191→  let game_mode_and_gm_multi = data.(3) <> 0 in
   192→
   193→  if player_num = player_num_global then begin
   194→    if guided_is_null then
   195→      (* C returns early here — Guided_missile[player_num] is already NULL *)
   196→      ()
   197→    else begin
   198→      Effect.perform (Release_guided_set_viewer player_num);
   199→      if game_mode_and_gm_multi then
   200→        Effect.perform (Release_guided_multi_send player_num);

200→        Effect.perform (Release_guided_multi_send player_num);
   201→      if newdemo_state = nd_state_recording then
   202→        Effect.perform Release_guided_newdemo_record;
   203→      (* Fall through to set Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
   204→      Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
   205→    end
   206→  end else
   207→    (* player_num != Player_num: just clear *)
   208→    Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
   209→;;
   210→
   211→(* delete_old_omega_blobs: Delete all omega weapon blobs belonging to the same parent.
   212→   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp delete_old_omega_blobs *)
   213→let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
   214→  let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
   215→  let count = Array.length objnums in
   216→  for i = 0 to count - 1 do
   217→    Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
   218→  done
   219→  (* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
   220→;;
   221→

if newdemo_state = nd_state_recording then
        Effect.perform Release_guided_newdemo_record;
      (* Fall through to set Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
    end
  end else
    (* player_num != Player_num: just clear *)
    Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
;;

(* delete_old_omega_blobs: Delete all omega weapon blobs belonging to the same parent.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp delete_old_omega_blobs *)
let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
  let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
  let count = Array.length objnums in
  for i = 0 to count - 1 do
    Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
  done
  (* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
;;

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Summary Report: Omega Blob Functions Analysis

I've completed a thorough search and analysis of the omega blob functions in the ChocolateDescent codebase. Here are my findings:

### **C Implementation** (`/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/laser.cpp`)

#### **Constants (Lines 296-316)**
- **MIN_OMEGA_BLOBS**: 3 — minimum blobs created regardless of distance
- **MIN_OMEGA_DIST**: F1_0 * 3 — minimum distance between blobs
- **DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST**: F1_0 * 5 — desired distance between blobs
- **MAX_OMEGA_BLOBS**: 16 — maximum number of blobs
- **MAX_OMEGA_DIST**: MAX_OMEGA_BLOBS * DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST
- **OMEGA_MIN_TRACKABLE_DOT**: 15*F1_0/16 — cone width for tracking
- **OMEGA_MAX_TRACKABLE_DIST**: MAX_OMEGA_DIST
- **OMEGA_DAMAGE_SCALE**: 32 — controls damage multiplier
- **OMEGA_ENERGY_CONSUMPTION**: 16 — controls energy consumption

#### **Globals (Lines 499, 503)**
- **Omega_charge**: fix (initialized to MAX_OMEGA_CHARGE = F1_0)
- **Last_omega_fire_frame**: int (initialized to 0)

#### **`delete_old_omega_blobs()` (Lines 319-363)**
- **Input**: `object *parent_objp`
- **Bridge status**: Already has USE_OX_BRIDGE with effect registration (Lines 321-346)
- **Logic**: 
  - Gets parent object number from parent laser info
  - Iterates all objects, finds those matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num)
  - Deletes each matching object via `obj_delete()`
  - Logs deletion count (debug output)
- **Effects used**: 
  - `Fetch_omega_blob_objnums`: returns array of matching object numbers
  - `Obj_delete`: deletes each object
- **Bridge registration function**: `cd_ox_register_delete_omega_blobs_effects()` takes two lambdas

#### **`create_omega_blobs()` (Lines 366-495)**
- **Inputs**: `int firing_segnum`, `vms_vector *firing_pos`, `vms_vector *goal_pos`, `object *parent_objp`
- **Logic flow**:
  1. If multiplayer, calls `delete_old_omega_blobs(parent_objp)`
  2. Computes vector from firing_pos to goal_pos
  3. Calculates blob count and spacing based on distance:
     - If distance < MIN_OMEGA_BLOBS * MIN_OMEGA_DIST: use MIN_OMEGA_DIST
     - Else: use DESIRED_OMEGA_DIST, cap to MAX_OMEGA_BLOBS
  4. Sets up perturbation array for blob spread
  5. Generates random perturbation vector
  6. For each blob (i=0 to num_omega_blobs-1):
     - Calculates blob position with perturbation
     - Finds segment containing position via `find_point_seg()`
     - Creates weapon object via `obj_create()` with:
       - type=OBJ_WEAPON, id=OMEGA_ID
       - lifeleft=ONE_FRAME_TIME
       - velocity scaled by F1_0*4
       - shields = (OMEGA_DAMAGE_SCALE * FrameTime) * weapon_strength
       - Laser info: parent_type, parent_sig, parent_num
       - movement_type=MT_NONE for all but last blob
  7. Final blob: scaled velocity by weapon_speed, movement_type=MT_PHYSICS

### **OCaml Implementation** (`/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_laser.ml`)

#### **Current Status (Lines 211-220)**
- **`delete_old_omega_blobs`**: Already implemented
  ```ocaml
  let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
    let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
    let count = Array.length objnums in
    for i = 0 to count - 1 do
      Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
    done
  ```
- **Status**: Complete and functional

#### **Effect Types Already Defined (Lines 40-43)**
- **Fetch_omega_blob_objnums**: `int -> int array` effect
- **Obj_delete**: `int -> unit` effect

#### **Available Math Functions** (from `ox_math.ml`)
All functions needed for create_omega_blobs are available:
- `vm_vec_sub` — vector subtraction
- `vm_vec_normalize_quick` — quick normalization
- `vm_vec_scale` — scale vector by scalar
- `vm_vec_scale_add` — scale and add
- `vm_vec_scale_add2` — in-place scale and add
- `fixmul` — fixed-point multiplication
- `fixdiv` — fixed-point division
- `make_random_vector` — already implemented in ox_laser.ml (lines 47-52)

#### **Available Segment Functions** (from `ox_gameseg.ml`)
- **`find_point_seg`** (line 776): Takes packed array, returns segment number
- **`find_point_seg_v2`** (line 926): Alternative version with explicit parameters

### **Bridge Layer** (`/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox-bridge/`)

#### **C Glue (`bridge.c` lines 8750-8790)**
- **Registration function**: `cd_ox_register_delete_omega_blobs_effects()` — stores callback pointers for effects
- **Effect implementations**:
  - `cd_ox_effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums()` — calls C external, marshals to OCaml array
  - `cd_ox_effect_obj_delete()` — calls C external
- **Entry point**: `cd_ox_delete_old_omega_blobs()` — invokes OCaml callback

#### **Bridge Header (`bridge.h` lines 1980-1988)**
- Declares the C entry point and registration function
- Typedefs for effect handlers

#### **OCaml Bridge (`laser.ml` lines 65-131)**
- External declarations for effect functions
- Effect handler in `effc` function that matches on effect types
- Handler continuation for both Fetch_omega_blob_objnums and Obj_delete

### **Key Dependencies**

#### **For `create_omega_blobs` implementation:**
1. **Effect for finding segments**: Need `Find_point_seg` effect to find segments by position
   - Currently: `find_point_seg` in ox_gameseg.ml uses packed array format
   - May need to create an effect wrapper OR use the packed array directly if accessible
   
2. **Effect for object creation**: Need `Obj_create` effect to create weapon objects
   - Not yet in laser effects — would need to add it
   - C code uses: `obj_create(OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, segnum, &temp_pos, NULL, 0, CT_WEAPON, MT_PHYSICS, RT_WEAPON_VCLIP)`

3. **Effect for setting object properties**: Partially covered by `Set_weapon_obj_props`
   - Covers model_num, size, mass, drag, phys_flags
   - May need additional effect for lifeleft and movement_type

4. **Effect for setting velocity and shields**: Not currently available
   - C code sets: `phys_info.velocity`, `size`, `shields`, `laser_info.*`, `movement_type`
   - Likely needs `Set_laser_object_props` effect

5. **Access to globals**: 
   - Frame multiplication (FrameTime)
   - Weapon_info array for blob_size, strength, speed
   - Game_mode (GM_MULTI check)
   - Difficulty_level

### **File Locations**

| File | Purpose | Lines |
|------|---------|-------|
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/laser.cpp` | C implementation | 295-495 |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/laser.h` | Constants & declarations | 40, 136-139 |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_laser.ml` | OCaml pure functions | 211-220 (delete_old_omega_blobs) |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox-bridge/laser.ml` | Effect handlers | 65-131 |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox-bridge/bridge.c` | C glue | 8750-8790 |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox-bridge/bridge.h` | C declarations | 1980-1988 |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_math.ml` | Vector math (available) | Lines with vm_vec_* |
| `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_gameseg.ml` | Segment functions | 776, 926 |

content.content:
1→(* Pure OCaml game logic for laser/missile functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d2/laser.cpp and main_d1/laser.cpp *)
     3→
     4→let f1_0 = 0x10000
     5→
     6→(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)
     7→
     8→type _ Effect.t +=
     9→  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
    10→  | Fetch_object_pos : int -> (int * int * int) Effect.t
    11→      (* objnum -> (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z) *)
    12→  | Laser_create_new :
    13→      (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int)
    14→      -> int Effect.t
    15→      (* (dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, segnum, parent_objnum,
    16→          objtype, make_sound) -> objnum or -1 *)
    17→  | Set_laser_track_goal : (int * int) -> unit Effect.t
    18→      (* (objnum, goal_obj) *)
    19→  | Fetch_weapon_create_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    20→      (* weapon_type -> [|render_type; blob_size; model_num;
    21→         po_len_to_width_ratio; mass; drag; bounce; bounce_cheat;
    22→         polygon_model_rad|] *)
    23→  | Obj_create_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
    24→      (* (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, laser_radius, rtype)
    25→         -> objnum *)
    26→  | Set_weapon_obj_props : int array -> unit Effect.t
    27→      (* [|objnum; model_num; size; mass; drag; phys_flags_or|]
    28→         model_num < 0 means skip polymodel setup
    29→         phys_flags_or is OR'd into existing flags *)
    30→  | Fetch_release_guided_missile_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    31→      (* player_num -> [| Player_num; guided_is_null; newdemo_state; game_mode_and_gm_multi |] *)
    32→  | Release_guided_set_viewer : int -> unit Effect.t
    33→      (* player_num -> sets Missile_viewer = Guided_missile[player_num] *)
    34→  | Release_guided_multi_send : int -> unit Effect.t
    35→      (* player_num -> multi_send_guided_info(Guided_missile[player_num], 1) *)
    36→  | Release_guided_newdemo_record : unit Effect.t
    37→      (* newdemo_record_guided_end() *)
    38→  | Release_guided_clear : int -> unit Effect.t
    39→      (* player_num -> Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
    40→  | Fetch_omega_blob_objnums : int -> int array Effect.t
    41→      (* parent_num -> array of objnums matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num) *)
    42→  | Obj_delete : int -> unit Effect.t
    43→      (* objnum -> deletes the object *)
    44→
    45→(* make_random_vector: generate a random unit-ish vector using P_Rand.
    46→   Same logic as C make_random_vector / ox_controlcen.ml *)
    47→let make_random_vector () =
    48→  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
    49→  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    50→  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    51→  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
    52→  v
    53→;;
    54→
    55→(* create_homing_missile: Create a homing missile aimed at goal_obj.
    56→   If goal_obj == -1, fire in a random direction.
    57→   D1 and D2 versions are functionally identical.
    58→   C original: laser.cpp create_homing_missile *)
    59→let create_homing_missile ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
    60→    ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound =
    61→  let vector_to_goal =
    62→    if goal_obj = -1 then
    63→      make_random_vector ()
    64→    else begin
    65→      let goal_x, goal_y, goal_z = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_pos goal_obj) in
    66→      (* D2 uses vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick = sub + normalize_quick
    67→         D1 uses vm_vec_sub + vm_vec_normalize_quick — same result *)
    68→      let _, dir =
    69→        Ox_math.vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick
    70→          ~v_end:(goal_x, goal_y, goal_z)
    71→          ~v_start:(objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z)
    72→      in
    73→      let random_vector = make_random_vector () in
    74→      let blended =
    75→        Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:dir ~src:random_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
    76→      in
    77→      let _, normalized = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:blended in
    78→      normalized
    79→    end
    80→  in
    81→  let dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = vector_to_goal in
    82→  let objnum =
    83→    Effect.perform
    84→      (Laser_create_new
    85→         ( dir_x, dir_y, dir_z
    86→         , objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z
    87→         , objp_segnum, objp_objnum, objtype, make_sound ))
    88→  in
    89→  if objnum = -1 then -1
    90→  else begin
    91→    Effect.perform (Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj));
    92→    objnum
    93→  end
    94→;;
    95→
    96→(* -- Constants for create_weapon_object ----------------------------------- *)
    97→
    98→let weapon_render_none = -1
    99→let weapon_render_laser = 0
   100→let weapon_render_blob = 1
   101→let weapon_render_polymodel = 2
   102→let weapon_render_vclip = 3
   103→
   104→let rt_none = 0
   105→let rt_polyobj = 1
   106→let rt_laser = 3
   107→let rt_weapon_vclip = 7
   108→
   109→let pf_bounce = 0x04
   110→let pf_bounces_twice = 0x200
   111→
   112→(* create_weapon_object: Create a weapon object with the right render type,
   113→   size, physics, and bounce properties.
   114→   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp create_weapon_object *)
   115→let create_weapon_object ~weapon_type ~segnum ~pos_x ~pos_y ~pos_z =
   116→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type) in
   117→  let render_type = data.(0) in
   118→  let blob_size = data.(1) in
   119→  let model_num = data.(2) in
   120→  let po_len_to_width_ratio = data.(3) in
   121→  let mass = data.(4) in
   122→  let drag = data.(5) in
   123→  let bounce = data.(6) in
   124→  let bounce_cheat = data.(7) in
   125→  let polygon_model_rad = data.(8) in
   126→
   127→  let rtype = ref (-1) in
   128→  let laser_radius = ref (-1) in
   129→
   130→  (match render_type with
   131→   | r when r = weapon_render_blob ->
   132→     rtype := rt_laser;
   133→     laser_radius := blob_size
   134→   | r when r = weapon_render_polymodel ->
   135→     laser_radius := 0;
   136→     rtype := rt_polyobj
   137→   | r when r = weapon_render_laser ->
   138→     () (* Int3() - not supported anymore *)
   139→   | r when r = weapon_render_none ->
   140→     rtype := rt_none;
   141→     laser_radius := f1_0
   142→   | r when r = weapon_render_vclip ->
   143→     rtype := rt_weapon_vclip;
   144→     laser_radius := blob_size
   145→   | _ ->
   146→     failwith "Invalid weapon render type in Laser_create_new");
   147→
   148→  (* Assert(laser_radius != -1); Assert(rtype != -1); *)
   149→
   150→  let objnum = Effect.perform
   151→    (Obj_create_weapon
   152→       (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, !laser_radius, !rtype))
   153→  in
   154→
   155→  (* Compute size for polymodel weapons *)
   156→  let final_size =
   157→    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then
   158→      Ox_math.fixdiv ~a:polygon_model_rad ~b:po_len_to_width_ratio
   159→    else
   160→      0 (* not used — obj_create already set size to laser_radius *)
   161→  in
   162→
   163→  let final_model_num =
   164→    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then model_num else -1
   165→  in
   166→
   167→  (* Compute phys_flags to OR in *)
   168→  let phys_flags = ref 0 in
   169→  if bounce = 1 then
   170→    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor pf_bounce;
   171→  if bounce = 2 || bounce_cheat <> 0 then
   172→    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor (pf_bounce + pf_bounces_twice);
   173→
   174→  Effect.perform
   175→    (Set_weapon_obj_props
   176→       [| objnum; final_model_num; final_size; mass; drag; !phys_flags |]);
   177→
   178→  objnum
   179→;;
   180→
   181→(* ND_STATE_RECORDING = 1 *)
   182→let nd_state_recording = 1
   183→
   184→(* release_guided_missile: give up control of the guided missile.
   185→   C original: laser.cpp release_guided_missile *)
   186→let release_guided_missile ~player_num =
   187→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num) in
   188→  let player_num_global = data.(0) in
   189→  let guided_is_null = data.(1) <> 0 in
   190→  let newdemo_state = data.(2) in
   191→  let game_mode_and_gm_multi = data.(3) <> 0 in
   192→
   193→  if player_num = player_num_global then begin
   194→    if guided_is_null then
   195→      (* C returns early here — Guided_missile[player_num] is already NULL *)
   196→      ()
   197→    else begin
   198→      Effect.perform (Release_guided_set_viewer player_num);
   199→      if game_mode_and_gm_multi then
   200→        Effect.perform (Release_guided_multi_send player_num);
   201→      if newdemo_state = nd_state_recording then
   202→        Effect.perform Release_guided_newdemo_record;
   203→      (* Fall through to set Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
   204→      Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
   205→    end
   206→  end else
   207→    (* player_num != Player_num: just clear *)
   208→    Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
   209→;;
   210→
   211→(* delete_old_omega_blobs: Delete all omega weapon blobs belonging to the same parent.
   212→   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp delete_old_omega_blobs *)
   213→let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
   214→  let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
   215→  let count = Array.length objnums in
   216→  for i = 0 to count - 1 do
   217→    Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
   218→  done
   219→  (* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
   220→;;

(* Pure OCaml game logic for laser/missile functions.
   Ported from main_d2/laser.cpp and main_d1/laser.cpp *)

let f1_0 = 0x10000

(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)

type _ Effect.t +=
  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
  | Fetch_object_pos : int -> (int * int * int) Effect.t
      (* objnum -> (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z) *)
  | Laser_create_new :
      (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int)
      -> int Effect.t
      (* (dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, segnum, parent_objnum,
          objtype, make_sound) -> objnum or -1 *)
  | Set_laser_track_goal : (int * int) -> unit Effect.t
      (* (objnum, goal_obj) *)
  | Fetch_weapon_create_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* weapon_type -> [|render_type; blob_size; model_num;
         po_len_to_width_ratio; mass; drag; bounce; bounce_cheat;
         polygon_model_rad|] *)
  | Obj_create_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
      (* (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, laser_radius, rtype)
         -> objnum *)
  | Set_weapon_obj_props : int array -> unit Effect.t
      (* [|objnum; model_num; size; mass; drag; phys_flags_or|]
         model_num < 0 means skip polymodel setup
         phys_flags_or is OR'd into existing flags *)
  | Fetch_release_guided_missile_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* player_num -> [| Player_num; guided_is_null; newdemo_state; game_mode_and_gm_multi |] *)
  | Release_guided_set_viewer : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> sets Missile_viewer = Guided_missile[player_num] *)
  | Release_guided_multi_send : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> multi_send_guided_info(Guided_missile[player_num], 1) *)
  | Release_guided_newdemo_record : unit Effect.t
      (* newdemo_record_guided_end() *)
  | Release_guided_clear : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
  | Fetch_omega_blob_objnums : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* parent_num -> array of objnums matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num) *)
  | Obj_delete : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* objnum -> deletes the object *)

(* make_random_vector: generate a random unit-ish vector using P_Rand.
   Same logic as C make_random_vector / ox_controlcen.ml *)
let make_random_vector () =
  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
  v
;;

(* create_homing_missile: Create a homing missile aimed at goal_obj.
   If goal_obj == -1, fire in a random direction.
   D1 and D2 versions are functionally identical.
   C original: laser.cpp create_homing_missile *)
let create_homing_missile ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
    ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound =
  let vector_to_goal =
    if goal_obj = -1 then
      make_random_vector ()
    else begin
      let goal_x, goal_y, goal_z = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_pos goal_obj) in
      (* D2 uses vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick = sub + normalize_quick
         D1 uses vm_vec_sub + vm_vec_normalize_quick — same result *)
      let _, dir =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick
          ~v_end:(goal_x, goal_y, goal_z)
          ~v_start:(objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z)
      in
      let random_vector = make_random_vector () in
      let blended =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:dir ~src:random_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
      in
      let _, normalized = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:blended in
      normalized
    end
  in
  let dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = vector_to_goal in
  let objnum =
    Effect.perform
      (Laser_create_new
         ( dir_x, dir_y, dir_z
         , objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z
         , objp_segnum, objp_objnum, objtype, make_sound ))
  in
  if objnum = -1 then -1
  else begin
    Effect.perform (Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj));
    objnum
  end
;;

(* -- Constants for create_weapon_object ----------------------------------- *)

let weapon_render_none = -1
let weapon_render_laser = 0
let weapon_render_blob = 1
let weapon_render_polymodel = 2
let weapon_render_vclip = 3

let rt_none = 0
let rt_polyobj = 1
let rt_laser = 3
let rt_weapon_vclip = 7

let pf_bounce = 0x04
let pf_bounces_twice = 0x200

(* create_weapon_object: Create a weapon object with the right render type,
   size, physics, and bounce properties.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp create_weapon_object *)
let create_weapon_object ~weapon_type ~segnum ~pos_x ~pos_y ~pos_z =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type) in
  let render_type = data.(0) in
  let blob_size = data.(1) in
  let model_num = data.(2) in
  let po_len_to_width_ratio = data.(3) in
  let mass = data.(4) in
  let drag = data.(5) in
  let bounce = data.(6) in
  let bounce_cheat = data.(7) in
  let polygon_model_rad = data.(8) in

  let rtype = ref (-1) in
  let laser_radius = ref (-1) in

  (match render_type with
   | r when r = weapon_render_blob ->
     rtype := rt_laser;
     laser_radius := blob_size
   | r when r = weapon_render_polymodel ->
     laser_radius := 0;
     rtype := rt_polyobj
   | r when r = weapon_render_laser ->
     () (* Int3() - not supported anymore *)
   | r when r = weapon_render_none ->
     rtype := rt_none;
     laser_radius := f1_0
   | r when r = weapon_render_vclip ->
     rtype := rt_weapon_vclip;
     laser_radius := blob_size
   | _ ->
     failwith "Invalid weapon render type in Laser_create_new");

  (* Assert(laser_radius != -1); Assert(rtype != -1); *)

  let objnum = Effect.perform
    (Obj_create_weapon
       (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, !laser_radius, !rtype))
  in

  (* Compute size for polymodel weapons *)
  let final_size =
    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then
      Ox_math.fixdiv ~a:polygon_model_rad ~b:po_len_to_width_ratio
    else
      0 (* not used — obj_create already set size to laser_radius *)
  in

  let final_model_num =
    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then model_num else -1
  in

  (* Compute phys_flags to OR in *)
  let phys_flags = ref 0 in
  if bounce = 1 then
    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor pf_bounce;
  if bounce = 2 || bounce_cheat <> 0 then
    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor (pf_bounce + pf_bounces_twice);

  Effect.perform
    (Set_weapon_obj_props
       [| objnum; final_model_num; final_size; mass; drag; !phys_flags |]);

  objnum
;;

(* ND_STATE_RECORDING = 1 *)
let nd_state_recording = 1

(* release_guided_missile: give up control of the guided missile.
   C original: laser.cpp release_guided_missile *)
let release_guided_missile ~player_num =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num) in
  let player_num_global = data.(0) in
  let guided_is_null = data.(1) <> 0 in
  let newdemo_state = data.(2) in
  let game_mode_and_gm_multi = data.(3) <> 0 in

  if player_num = player_num_global then begin
    if guided_is_null then
      (* C returns early here — Guided_missile[player_num] is already NULL *)
      ()
    else begin
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_set_viewer player_num);
      if game_mode_and_gm_multi then
        Effect.perform (Release_guided_multi_send player_num);
      if newdemo_state = nd_state_recording then
        Effect.perform Release_guided_newdemo_record;
      (* Fall through to set Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
    end
  end else
    (* player_num != Player_num: just clear *)
    Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
;;

(* delete_old_omega_blobs: Delete all omega weapon blobs belonging to the same parent.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp delete_old_omega_blobs *)
let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
  let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
  let count = Array.length objnums in
  for i = 0 to count - 1 do
    Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
  done
  (* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
;;

   189→        Out_channel.flush stderr;

(* Bridge adapter for laser/missile functions.
   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)

(* Effect externals *)
external effect_fetch_object_pos
  :  int
  -> int array
  = "cd_ox_effect_laser_fetch_object_pos"

external effect_laser_create_new
  :  int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int
  -> int
  = "cd_ox_effect_laser_create_new_bytecode"
    "cd_ox_effect_laser_create_new"

external effect_set_laser_track_goal
  :  int -> int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_laser_set_track_goal"

external effect_p_rand : unit -> int = "cd_ox_effect_p_rand"

external effect_fetch_weapon_create_data
  :  int
  -> int array
  = "cd_ox_effect_fetch_weapon_create_data"

external effect_obj_create_weapon
  :  int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int
  -> int
  = "cd_ox_effect_obj_create_weapon_bytecode"
    "cd_ox_effect_obj_create_weapon"

external effect_set_weapon_obj_props
  :  int -> int -> int -> int -> int -> int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_set_weapon_obj_props_bytecode"
    "cd_ox_effect_set_weapon_obj_props"

external effect_fetch_release_guided_missile_data
  :  int
  -> int array
  = "cd_ox_effect_fetch_release_guided_missile_data"

external effect_release_guided_set_viewer
  :  int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_set_viewer"

external effect_release_guided_multi_send
  :  int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_multi_send"

external effect_release_guided_newdemo_record
  :  unit
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_newdemo_record"

external effect_release_guided_clear
  :  int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_release_guided_clear"

external effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums
  :  int
  -> int array
  = "cd_ox_effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums"

external effect_obj_delete
  :  int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_obj_delete"

(* Effect handler *)
let effc (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) : ((a, 'b) Effect.Deep.continuation -> 'b) option =
  match eff with
  | Ox_laser.P_Rand ->
    Some (fun k -> Effect.Deep.continue k (effect_p_rand ()))
  | Ox_laser.Fetch_object_pos objnum ->
    Some (fun k ->
      let arr = effect_fetch_object_pos objnum in
      Effect.Deep.continue k (arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2)))
  | Ox_laser.Laser_create_new (dx, dy, dz, px, py, pz, segnum, parent, objtype, make_sound) ->
    Some (fun k ->
      let objnum = effect_laser_create_new dx dy dz px py pz segnum parent objtype make_sound in
      Effect.Deep.continue k objnum)
  | Ox_laser.Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj) ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_set_laser_track_goal objnum goal_obj;
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_laser.Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type ->
    Some (fun k ->
      let arr = effect_fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type in
      Effect.Deep.continue k arr)
  | Ox_laser.Obj_create_weapon (wtype, segnum, px, py, pz, radius, rtype) ->
    Some (fun k ->
      let objnum = effect_obj_create_weapon wtype segnum px py pz radius rtype in
      Effect.Deep.continue k objnum)
  | Ox_laser.Set_weapon_obj_props arr ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_set_weapon_obj_props arr.(0) arr.(1) arr.(2) arr.(3) arr.(4) arr.(5);
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_laser.Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num ->
    Some (fun k ->
      let arr = effect_fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num in
      Effect.Deep.continue k arr)
  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_set_viewer player_num ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_release_guided_set_viewer player_num;
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_multi_send player_num ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_release_guided_multi_send player_num;
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_newdemo_record ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_release_guided_newdemo_record ();
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_laser.Release_guided_clear player_num ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_release_guided_clear player_num;
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_laser.Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num ->
    Some (fun k ->
      let arr = effect_fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num in
      Effect.Deep.continue k arr)
  | Ox_laser.Obj_delete objnum ->
    Some (fun k ->
      effect_obj_delete objnum;
      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | _ -> None
;;

let cd_create_homing_missile objp_pos_x objp_pos_y objp_pos_z
    objp_segnum objp_objnum goal_obj objtype make_sound =
  Effect.Deep.match_with
    (fun () ->
      Ox_laser.create_homing_missile
        ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
        ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound)
    ()
    { retc = (fun r -> r)
    ; exnc = (fun e ->
        Printf.eprintf "[OX] create_homing_missile exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
        Out_channel.flush stderr;
        -1)
    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    }
;;

let cd_create_weapon_object weapon_type segnum pos_x pos_y pos_z =
  Effect.Deep.match_with
    (fun () ->
      Ox_laser.create_weapon_object
        ~weapon_type ~segnum ~pos_x ~pos_y ~pos_z)
    ()
    { retc = (fun r -> r)
    ; exnc = (fun e ->
        Printf.eprintf "[OX] create_weapon_object exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
        Out_channel.flush stderr;
        -1)
    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    }
;;

let cd_release_guided_missile player_num =
  Effect.Deep.match_with
    (fun () ->
      Ox_laser.release_guided_missile ~player_num)
    ()
    { retc = (fun () -> ())
    ; exnc = (fun e ->
        Printf.eprintf "[OX] release_guided_missile exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
        Out_channel.flush stderr;
        ())
    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    }
;;

let cd_delete_old_omega_blobs parent_num =
  Effect.Deep.match_with
    (fun () ->
      Ox_laser.delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num)
    ()
    { retc = (fun () -> ())
    ; exnc = (fun e ->
        Printf.eprintf "[OX] delete_old_omega_blobs exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
        Out_channel.flush stderr;
        ())
    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    }
;;

let register_callbacks () =
  Callback.register "cd_create_homing_missile" cd_create_homing_missile;
  Callback.register "cd_create_weapon_object" cd_create_weapon_object;
  Callback.register "cd_release_guided_missile" cd_release_guided_missile;
  Callback.register "cd_delete_old_omega_blobs" cd_delete_old_omega_blobs
;;

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate LLVM call graphs for Descent game logic files.

Uses compile_commands.json to get exact flags, compiles to LLVM IR,
runs opt -dot-callgraph, pipes through llvm-cxxfilt to demangle,
then merges into a unified graph with porting status annotations.

Usage:
    python3 scripts/gen_callgraph.py [--render]

Output:
    callgraph/                     - per-file IR and DOT files
    callgraph/unified.dot          - merged call graph with porting annotations
    callgraph/unified.svg          - rendered SVG (if --render and graphviz installed)
    callgraph/porting.json         - machine-readable porting status + graph data
"""

import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path

PROJECT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
BUILD_DIR = PROJECT / "build-c-ref"
COMPILE_DB = BUILD_DIR / "compile_commands.json"
LLVM_PREFIX = subprocess.check_output(
    ["brew", "--prefix", "llvm@20"], text=True
).strip()
CLANG = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/clang++"
OPT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/opt"
CXXFILT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/llvm-cxxfilt"
OUT_DIR = PROJECT / "callgraph"

# Core game logic files we care about for the port
GAME_LOGIC_FILES = [
    "main_d2/ai.cpp",
    "main_d2/ai2.cpp",
    "main_d2/aipath.cpp",
    "main_d2/physics.cpp",

]

# "authoritative" = OCaml drives the game, C path skipped
# "ported" = fully implemented in OCaml, called via bridge
# Source of truth: grep for cd_ox_ calls in main_d2/*.cpp

(* Pure OCaml game logic for laser/missile functions.
   Ported from main_d2/laser.cpp and main_d1/laser.cpp *)

let f1_0 = 0x10000

(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)

type _ Effect.t +=
  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
  | Fetch_object_pos : int -> (int * int * int) Effect.t
      (* objnum -> (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z) *)
  | Laser_create_new :
      (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int)
      -> int Effect.t
      (* (dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, segnum, parent_objnum,
          objtype, make_sound) -> objnum or -1 *)
  | Set_laser_track_goal : (int * int) -> unit Effect.t
      (* (objnum, goal_obj) *)
  | Fetch_weapon_create_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* weapon_type -> [|render_type; blob_size; model_num;
         po_len_to_width_ratio; mass; drag; bounce; bounce_cheat;
         polygon_model_rad|] *)
  | Obj_create_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
      (* (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, laser_radius, rtype)
         -> objnum *)
  | Set_weapon_obj_props : int array -> unit Effect.t
      (* [|objnum; model_num; size; mass; drag; phys_flags_or|]
         model_num < 0 means skip polymodel setup
         phys_flags_or is OR'd into existing flags *)
  | Fetch_release_guided_missile_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* player_num -> [| Player_num; guided_is_null; newdemo_state; game_mode_and_gm_multi |] *)
  | Release_guided_set_viewer : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> sets Missile_viewer = Guided_missile[player_num] *)
  | Release_guided_multi_send : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> multi_send_guided_info(Guided_missile[player_num], 1) *)
  | Release_guided_newdemo_record : unit Effect.t
      (* newdemo_record_guided_end() *)
  | Release_guided_clear : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
  | Fetch_omega_blob_objnums : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* parent_num -> array of objnums matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num) *)
  | Obj_delete : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* objnum -> deletes the object *)

(* make_random_vector: generate a random unit-ish vector using P_Rand.
   Same logic as C make_random_vector / ox_controlcen.ml *)
let make_random_vector () =
  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
  v
;;

(* create_homing_missile: Create a homing missile aimed at goal_obj.
   If goal_obj == -1, fire in a random direction.
   D1 and D2 versions are functionally identical.
   C original: laser.cpp create_homing_missile *)
let create_homing_missile ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
    ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound =
  let vector_to_goal =
    if goal_obj = -1 then
      make_random_vector ()
    else begin
      let goal_x, goal_y, goal_z = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_pos goal_obj) in
      (* D2 uses vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick = sub + normalize_quick
         D1 uses vm_vec_sub + vm_vec_normalize_quick — same result *)
      let _, dir =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick
          ~v_end:(goal_x, goal_y, goal_z)
          ~v_start:(objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z)
      in
      let random_vector = make_random_vector () in
      let blended =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:dir ~src:random_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
      in
      let _, normalized = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:blended in
      normalized
    end
  in
  let dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = vector_to_goal in
  let objnum =
    Effect.perform
      (Laser_create_new
         ( dir_x, dir_y, dir_z
         , objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z
         , objp_segnum, objp_objnum, objtype, make_sound ))
  in
  if objnum = -1 then -1
  else begin
    Effect.perform (Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj));
    objnum
  end
;;

(* -- Constants for create_weapon_object ----------------------------------- *)

let weapon_render_none = -1
let weapon_render_laser = 0
let weapon_render_blob = 1
let weapon_render_polymodel = 2
let weapon_render_vclip = 3

let rt_none = 0
let rt_polyobj = 1
let rt_laser = 3
let rt_weapon_vclip = 7

let pf_bounce = 0x04
let pf_bounces_twice = 0x200

(* create_weapon_object: Create a weapon object with the right render type,
   size, physics, and bounce properties.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp create_weapon_object *)
let create_weapon_object ~weapon_type ~segnum ~pos_x ~pos_y ~pos_z =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type) in
  let render_type = data.(0) in
  let blob_size = data.(1) in
  let model_num = data.(2) in
  let po_len_to_width_ratio = data.(3) in
  let mass = data.(4) in
  let drag = data.(5) in
  let bounce = data.(6) in
  let bounce_cheat = data.(7) in
  let polygon_model_rad = data.(8) in

  let rtype = ref (-1) in
  let laser_radius = ref (-1) in

  (match render_type with
   | r when r = weapon_render_blob ->
     rtype := rt_laser;
     laser_radius := blob_size
   | r when r = weapon_render_polymodel ->
     laser_radius := 0;
     rtype := rt_polyobj
   | r when r = weapon_render_laser ->
     () (* Int3() - not supported anymore *)
   | r when r = weapon_render_none ->
     rtype := rt_none;
     laser_radius := f1_0
   | r when r = weapon_render_vclip ->
     rtype := rt_weapon_vclip;
     laser_radius := blob_size
   | _ ->
     failwith "Invalid weapon render type in Laser_create_new");

  (* Assert(laser_radius != -1); Assert(rtype != -1); *)

  let objnum = Effect.perform
    (Obj_create_weapon
       (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, !laser_radius, !rtype))
  in

  (* Compute size for polymodel weapons *)
  let final_size =
    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then
      Ox_math.fixdiv ~a:polygon_model_rad ~b:po_len_to_width_ratio
    else
      0 (* not used — obj_create already set size to laser_radius *)
  in

  let final_model_num =
    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then model_num else -1
  in

  (* Compute phys_flags to OR in *)
  let phys_flags = ref 0 in
  if bounce = 1 then
    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor pf_bounce;
  if bounce = 2 || bounce_cheat <> 0 then
    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor (pf_bounce + pf_bounces_twice);

  Effect.perform
    (Set_weapon_obj_props
       [| objnum; final_model_num; final_size; mass; drag; !phys_flags |]);

  objnum
;;

(* ND_STATE_RECORDING = 1 *)
let nd_state_recording = 1

(* release_guided_missile: give up control of the guided missile.
   C original: laser.cpp release_guided_missile *)
let release_guided_missile ~player_num =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num) in
  let player_num_global = data.(0) in
  let guided_is_null = data.(1) <> 0 in
  let newdemo_state = data.(2) in
  let game_mode_and_gm_multi = data.(3) <> 0 in

  if player_num = player_num_global then begin
    if guided_is_null then
      (* C returns early here — Guided_missile[player_num] is already NULL *)
      ()
    else begin
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_set_viewer player_num);
      if game_mode_and_gm_multi then
        Effect.perform (Release_guided_multi_send player_num);
      if newdemo_state = nd_state_recording then
        Effect.perform Release_guided_newdemo_record;
      (* Fall through to set Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
    end
  end else
    (* player_num != Player_num: just clear *)
    Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
;;

(* delete_old_omega_blobs: Delete all omega weapon blobs belonging to the same parent.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp delete_old_omega_blobs *)
let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
  let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
  let count = Array.length objnums in
  for i = 0 to count - 1 do
    Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
  done
  (* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
;;

input.new_string:
(* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
;;

(* -- Constants for create_omega_blobs ------------------------------------- *)

let min_omega_blobs = 3
let min_omega_dist = f1_0 * 3
let desired_omega_dist = f1_0 * 5
let max_omega_blobs = 16
(* max_omega_dist = max_omega_blobs * desired_omega_dist *)
let omega_damage_scale = 32
let one_frame_time = 0x3ffffffe
let mt_none = 0
let mt_physics = 1

(* create_omega_blobs: Create a stream of omega weapon blobs from firing_pos
   to goal_pos. Blobs spread with random perturbation and live for one frame.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp create_omega_blobs *)
let create_omega_blobs ~firing_segnum ~fp_x ~fp_y ~fp_z
    ~gp_x ~gp_y ~gp_z ~parent_objnum =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_create_omega_data parent_objnum) in
  let is_multi = data.(0) <> 0 in
  let frame_time = data.(1) in
  let parent_type = data.(2) in
  let parent_sig = data.(3) in
  let parent_laser_parent_num = data.(4) in
  let parent_uvec_x = data.(5) in
  let parent_uvec_y = data.(6) in
  let parent_uvec_z = data.(7) in
  let blob_size = data.(8) in
  let omega_strength = data.(9) in
  let omega_speed = data.(10) in

  if is_multi then
    delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num:parent_laser_parent_num;

  let vec_to_goal_raw = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~v0:(gp_x, gp_y, gp_z) ~v1:(fp_x, fp_y, fp_z) in
  let dist_to_goal, vec_to_goal = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:vec_to_goal_raw in

  let num_omega_blobs = ref 0 in
  let omega_blob_dist = ref 0 in

  if dist_to_goal < min_omega_blobs * min_omega_dist then begin
    omega_blob_dist := min_omega_dist;
    num_omega_blobs := dist_to_goal / !omega_blob_dist;
    if !num_omega_blobs = 0 then
      num_omega_blobs := 1
  end else begin
    omega_blob_dist := desired_omega_dist;
    num_omega_blobs := dist_to_goal / !omega_blob_dist;
    if !num_omega_blobs > max_omega_blobs then begin
      num_omega_blobs := max_omega_blobs;
      omega_blob_dist := dist_to_goal / !num_omega_blobs
    end else if !num_omega_blobs < min_omega_blobs then begin
      num_omega_blobs := min_omega_blobs;
      omega_blob_dist := dist_to_goal / !num_omega_blobs
    end
  end;

  let vtg_x, vtg_y, vtg_z = vec_to_goal in
  let omega_delta_vector =
    Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:vec_to_goal ~s:(!omega_blob_dist)
  in

  (* Set up blob positions and perturbation *)
  let blob_pos = ref (fp_x, fp_y, fp_z) in
  let last_segnum = ref firing_segnum in

  let perturb_array = Array.make max_omega_blobs 0 in

  if dist_to_goal < min_omega_dist * 4 then begin
    (* Nearby: no perturbation *)
    for i = 0 to !num_omega_blobs - 1 do
      perturb_array.(i) <- 0
    done
  end else begin
    (* Not nearby: start halfway out *)
    blob_pos := Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:(!blob_pos) ~src:omega_delta_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 2);
    for i = 0 to !num_omega_blobs / 2 - 1 do
      perturb_array.(i) <- f1_0 * i + f1_0 / 4;
      perturb_array.(!num_omega_blobs - 1 - i) <- f1_0 * i
    done
  end;

  (* Create random perturbation vector, favor not going up in player's reference *)
  let perturb_vec = ref (make_random_vector ()) in
  perturb_vec := Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:(!perturb_vec)
    ~src:(parent_uvec_x, parent_uvec_y, parent_uvec_z) ~k:(- f1_0 / 2);

  Effect.perform (Set_doing_lighting_hack 1);

  let last_created_objnum = ref (-1) in
  let break_loop = ref false in

  for i = 0 to !num_omega_blobs - 1 do
    if not !break_loop then begin
      (* Last blob: move another almost half section *)
      if i = !num_omega_blobs - 1 then
        blob_pos := Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:(!blob_pos) ~src:omega_delta_vector ~k:(15 * f1_0 / 32);

      (* Every 4th blob (i%4==3), re-perturb *)
      if i mod 4 = 3 then begin
        let temp_vec = make_random_vector () in
        perturb_vec := Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:(!perturb_vec) ~src:temp_vec ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
      end;

      let temp_pos = Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add ~dest:(!blob_pos) ~src:(!perturb_vec) ~k:perturb_array.(i) in
      let tp_x, tp_y, tp_z = temp_pos in

      let segnum = Effect.perform (Find_point_seg_laser (tp_x, tp_y, tp_z, !last_segnum)) in
      if segnum <> -1 then begin
        last_segnum := segnum;

        (* Compute velocity: vec_to_goal scaled by F1_0*4 *)
        let vel = Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:vec_to_goal ~s:(f1_0 * 4) in
        let vel_x, vel_y, vel_z = vel in

        (* Compute shields: fixmul(OMEGA_DAMAGE_SCALE*FrameTime, strength) *)
        let shields = Ox_math.fixmul ~a:(omega_damage_scale * frame_time) ~b:omega_strength in

        let blob_objnum = Effect.perform (Create_omega_blob_obj
          [| segnum; tp_x; tp_y; tp_z; one_frame_time;
             vel_x; vel_y; vel_z; blob_size; shields;
             parent_type; parent_sig; parent_objnum; mt_none |])
        in
        if blob_objnum = -1 then
          break_loop := true
        else
          last_created_objnum := blob_objnum
      end;

      (* Advance blob_pos by omega_delta_vector *)
      let bx, by, bz = !blob_pos in
      let dx, dy, dz = omega_delta_vector in
      blob_pos := (bx + dx, by + dy, bz + dz)
    end
  done;

  (* Make last blob move faster: scale velocity by omega_speed/4, set MT_PHYSICS *)
  if !last_created_objnum <> -1 then begin
    (* The blob already has velocity = vec_to_goal * F1_0*4.
       Now scale that by omega_speed/4. *)
    let scaled_vel = Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:vec_to_goal ~s:(f1_0 * 4) in
    let sv_x, sv_y, sv_z = scaled_vel in
    let final_vx = Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sv_x ~b:(omega_speed / 4) in
    let final_vy = Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sv_y ~b:(omega_speed / 4) in
    let final_vz = Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sv_z ~b:(omega_speed / 4) in
    Effect.perform (Set_omega_blob_final [| !last_created_objnum; final_vx; final_vy; final_vz |])
  end;

  Effect.perform (Set_doing_lighting_hack 0)
;;

tool_use_result.originalFile:
(* Pure OCaml game logic for laser/missile functions.
   Ported from main_d2/laser.cpp and main_d1/laser.cpp *)

let f1_0 = 0x10000

(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)

type _ Effect.t +=
  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
  | Fetch_object_pos : int -> (int * int * int) Effect.t
      (* objnum -> (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z) *)
  | Laser_create_new :
      (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int)
      -> int Effect.t
      (* (dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, segnum, parent_objnum,
          objtype, make_sound) -> objnum or -1 *)
  | Set_laser_track_goal : (int * int) -> unit Effect.t
      (* (objnum, goal_obj) *)
  | Fetch_weapon_create_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* weapon_type -> [|render_type; blob_size; model_num;
         po_len_to_width_ratio; mass; drag; bounce; bounce_cheat;
         polygon_model_rad|] *)
  | Obj_create_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
      (* (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, laser_radius, rtype)
         -> objnum *)
  | Set_weapon_obj_props : int array -> unit Effect.t
      (* [|objnum; model_num; size; mass; drag; phys_flags_or|]
         model_num < 0 means skip polymodel setup
         phys_flags_or is OR'd into existing flags *)
  | Fetch_release_guided_missile_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* player_num -> [| Player_num; guided_is_null; newdemo_state; game_mode_and_gm_multi |] *)
  | Release_guided_set_viewer : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> sets Missile_viewer = Guided_missile[player_num] *)
  | Release_guided_multi_send : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> multi_send_guided_info(Guided_missile[player_num], 1) *)
  | Release_guided_newdemo_record : unit Effect.t
      (* newdemo_record_guided_end() *)
  | Release_guided_clear : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* player_num -> Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
  | Fetch_omega_blob_objnums : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* parent_num -> array of objnums matching (OBJ_WEAPON, OMEGA_ID, parent_num) *)
  | Obj_delete : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* objnum -> deletes the object *)
  | Fetch_create_omega_data : int -> int array Effect.t
      (* parent_objnum -> [| is_multi; frame_time; parent_type; parent_sig;
         parent_laser_parent_num; parent_uvec_x; parent_uvec_y; parent_uvec_z;
         blob_size; omega_strength; omega_speed |] *)
  | Find_point_seg_laser : (int * int * int * int) -> int Effect.t
      (* (pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, hint_seg) -> segnum *)
  | Create_omega_blob_obj : int array -> int Effect.t
      (* [| segnum; pos_x; pos_y; pos_z; lifeleft; vel_x; vel_y; vel_z;
         size; shields; parent_type; parent_sig; parent_num; movement_type |]
         -> objnum *)
  | Set_omega_blob_final : int array -> unit Effect.t
      (* [| objnum; vel_x; vel_y; vel_z |] — sets velocity and movement_type=MT_PHYSICS *)
  | Set_doing_lighting_hack : int -> unit Effect.t
      (* 0 or 1 -> sets Doing_lighting_hack_flag *)

(* make_random_vector: generate a random unit-ish vector using P_Rand.
   Same logic as C make_random_vector / ox_controlcen.ml *)
let make_random_vector () =
  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
  v
;;

(* create_homing_missile: Create a homing missile aimed at goal_obj.
   If goal_obj == -1, fire in a random direction.
   D1 and D2 versions are functionally identical.
   C original: laser.cpp create_homing_missile *)
let create_homing_missile ~objp_pos_x ~objp_pos_y ~objp_pos_z
    ~objp_segnum ~objp_objnum ~goal_obj ~objtype ~make_sound =
  let vector_to_goal =
    if goal_obj = -1 then
      make_random_vector ()
    else begin
      let goal_x, goal_y, goal_z = Effect.perform (Fetch_object_pos goal_obj) in
      (* D2 uses vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick = sub + normalize_quick
         D1 uses vm_vec_sub + vm_vec_normalize_quick — same result *)
      let _, dir =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_normalized_dir_quick
          ~v_end:(goal_x, goal_y, goal_z)
          ~v_start:(objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z)
      in
      let random_vector = make_random_vector () in
      let blended =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2 ~dest:dir ~src:random_vector ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
      in
      let _, normalized = Ox_math.vm_vec_normalize_quick ~v:blended in
      normalized
    end
  in
  let dir_x, dir_y, dir_z = vector_to_goal in
  let objnum =
    Effect.perform
      (Laser_create_new
         ( dir_x, dir_y, dir_z
         , objp_pos_x, objp_pos_y, objp_pos_z
         , objp_segnum, objp_objnum, objtype, make_sound ))
  in
  if objnum = -1 then -1
  else begin
    Effect.perform (Set_laser_track_goal (objnum, goal_obj));
    objnum
  end
;;

(* -- Constants for create_weapon_object ----------------------------------- *)

let weapon_render_none = -1
let weapon_render_laser = 0
let weapon_render_blob = 1
let weapon_render_polymodel = 2
let weapon_render_vclip = 3

let rt_none = 0
let rt_polyobj = 1
let rt_laser = 3
let rt_weapon_vclip = 7

let pf_bounce = 0x04
let pf_bounces_twice = 0x200

(* create_weapon_object: Create a weapon object with the right render type,
   size, physics, and bounce properties.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp create_weapon_object *)
let create_weapon_object ~weapon_type ~segnum ~pos_x ~pos_y ~pos_z =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_weapon_create_data weapon_type) in
  let render_type = data.(0) in
  let blob_size = data.(1) in
  let model_num = data.(2) in
  let po_len_to_width_ratio = data.(3) in
  let mass = data.(4) in
  let drag = data.(5) in
  let bounce = data.(6) in
  let bounce_cheat = data.(7) in
  let polygon_model_rad = data.(8) in

  let rtype = ref (-1) in
  let laser_radius = ref (-1) in

  (match render_type with
   | r when r = weapon_render_blob ->
     rtype := rt_laser;
     laser_radius := blob_size
   | r when r = weapon_render_polymodel ->
     laser_radius := 0;
     rtype := rt_polyobj
   | r when r = weapon_render_laser ->
     () (* Int3() - not supported anymore *)
   | r when r = weapon_render_none ->
     rtype := rt_none;
     laser_radius := f1_0
   | r when r = weapon_render_vclip ->
     rtype := rt_weapon_vclip;
     laser_radius := blob_size
   | _ ->
     failwith "Invalid weapon render type in Laser_create_new");

  (* Assert(laser_radius != -1); Assert(rtype != -1); *)

  let objnum = Effect.perform
    (Obj_create_weapon
       (weapon_type, segnum, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, !laser_radius, !rtype))
  in

  (* Compute size for polymodel weapons *)
  let final_size =
    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then
      Ox_math.fixdiv ~a:polygon_model_rad ~b:po_len_to_width_ratio
    else
      0 (* not used — obj_create already set size to laser_radius *)
  in

  let final_model_num =
    if render_type = weapon_render_polymodel then model_num else -1
  in

  (* Compute phys_flags to OR in *)
  let phys_flags = ref 0 in
  if bounce = 1 then
    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor pf_bounce;
  if bounce = 2 || bounce_cheat <> 0 then
    phys_flags := !phys_flags lor (pf_bounce + pf_bounces_twice);

  Effect.perform
    (Set_weapon_obj_props
       [| objnum; final_model_num; final_size; mass; drag; !phys_flags |]);

  objnum
;;

(* ND_STATE_RECORDING = 1 *)
let nd_state_recording = 1

(* release_guided_missile: give up control of the guided missile.
   C original: laser.cpp release_guided_missile *)
let release_guided_missile ~player_num =
  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_release_guided_missile_data player_num) in
  let player_num_global = data.(0) in
  let guided_is_null = data.(1) <> 0 in
  let newdemo_state = data.(2) in
  let game_mode_and_gm_multi = data.(3) <> 0 in

  if player_num = player_num_global then begin
    if guided_is_null then
      (* C returns early here — Guided_missile[player_num] is already NULL *)
      ()
    else begin
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_set_viewer player_num);
      if game_mode_and_gm_multi then
        Effect.perform (Release_guided_multi_send player_num);
      if newdemo_state = nd_state_recording then
        Effect.perform Release_guided_newdemo_record;
      (* Fall through to set Guided_missile[player_num] = NULL *)
      Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
    end
  end else
    (* player_num != Player_num: just clear *)
    Effect.perform (Release_guided_clear player_num)
;;

(* delete_old_omega_blobs: Delete all omega weapon blobs belonging to the same parent.
   C original: main_d2/laser.cpp delete_old_omega_blobs *)
let delete_old_omega_blobs ~parent_num =
  let objnums = Effect.perform (Fetch_omega_blob_objnums parent_num) in
  let count = Array.length objnums in
  for i = 0 to count - 1 do
    Effect.perform (Obj_delete objnums.(i))
  done
  (* mprintf omitted — debug-only logging *)
;;
